items.scenario: Under load your service runs out of file handles / connections because some code paths raise before close() is called.
items.question: How do context managers fix this?
items.answer: Acquire the resource in a with statement so __exit__ closes it on every exit path, including exceptions. For SDK clients/pools, use their context-manager API (or contextlib) so connections are always returned/closed.

items.scenario: You wrote a @contextmanager that does cleanup after yield, but on errors the cleanup is skipped.
items.question: What did you miss?
items.answer: The cleanup must be in a finally block around the yield. Without try/finally, an exception in the with-block propagates past the post-yield code. Wrap it: try: yield finally: cleanup().

items.scenario: A teammate's context manager swallows all errors and bugs go unnoticed.
items.question: What is the likely cause?
items.answer: Its __exit__ returns True, which suppresses exceptions. Return a falsy value (or nothing) so exceptions propagate after cleanup; only return True when you deliberately intend to handle/ignore a specific exception.
